description: Canonical terminology bindings for VHIR resources. Maps each coded field to its preferred coding system(s) and the corresponding value set.

systems.name: AAHA Diagnostic Terms
systems.purpose: Companion-animal diagnoses and problem codes; primary coding system for Condition resources in dogs, cats, ferrets, and rabbits.
systems.licenseNote: Freely available for non-commercial use with attribution. Commercial redistribution requires AAHA permission.

systems.name: SNOMED CT Veterinary Extension
systems.purpose: Surgical, dental, diagnostic-imaging, and clinical-pathology procedure codes; secondary coding for conditions when AAHA terms are absent.
systems.licenseNote: Requires an active SNOMED CT affiliate license (free for most countries via SNOMED International member territories).

systems.name: VeNom Coding Project
systems.publisher: VeNom Coding Group (Royal Veterinary College et al.)
systems.purpose: Cross-species clinical terms for diagnoses and procedures; useful for multi-species practices and species not covered by AAHA (exotics, equine, food animals).
systems.licenseNote: Available under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0. Full code list available via the VeNom project website.

systems.name: Unified Code for Units of Measure (UCUM)
systems.purpose: Units for all quantity fields: observation values, dose quantities, volumes, weights, and durations.
systems.licenseNote: Free to use; copyright Regenstrief Institute. Redistribution requires attribution.

systems.name: National Drug File – Reference Terminology (NDF-RT)
systems.purpose: Drug classification and pharmacological class codes for MedicationRequest and MedicationAdministration; used in lieu of RxNorm where full drug identity is not required.
systems.licenseNote: Public domain (US government work). No license required.

systems.name: VHIR Vaccine Code Value Set
systems.purpose: Veterinary vaccine identifiers following AAHA/AAFP/AAEP vaccine category names. Defined in spec/valuesets/vaccine_codes.json.

systems.name: LOINC
systems.purpose: Optional secondary system for Observation codes when a LOINC term exists for the measured analyte (e.g. laboratory panels with human-medicine equivalents).
systems.licenseNote: Free to use with attribution. Redistribution of the full LOINC database requires a LOINC license.

bindings.notes: Use AAHA Diagnostic Terms for companion animals (dogs, cats, ferrets). Fall back to VeNom for exotics, equine, or food-animal species not covered by AAHA. SNOMED CT-VET is an acceptable alternative for any species.
SNOMED CT-VET is the preferred system for surgical, dental, imaging, and clinical-pathology procedures. VeNom provides a lighter-weight alternative for practices that lack SNOMED CT access.
Use the VHIR vitals catalog for vital signs, body condition scores, and wearable telemetry. Use LOINC for laboratory panels where a veterinary LOINC code exists.

bindings.description: Unit of measure for a quantitative result
bindings.notes: UCUM codes are required for all machine-processable quantity values. Include the human-readable unit string in valueQuantity.unit alongside the UCUM code.

bindings.description: Unit of measure for sub-observation components
bindings.notes: Same requirement as the top-level valueQuantity.system.

NDF-RT pharmacological class codes are used when a structured drug classification is desired. The medication.name free-text field is always accepted; the system+code pair is optional but recommended for analytics.
UCUM required for dose quantities (mg, mL, IU, etc.).
Use the VHIR vaccine code value set (spec/valuesets/vaccine_codes.json) which follows AAHA, AAFP, and AAEP vaccine category names.

bindings.description: Unit of measure for vaccine dose
bindings.notes: Typical values: mL for injectable vaccines.

bindingStrengths.required: Implementations MUST use a code from the specified value set. No other codes are permitted.
bindingStrengths.preferred: Implementations SHOULD use a code from the specified value set. Deviation must be documented.
bindingStrengths.example: Implementations MAY use a code from the specified value set. Free text is also acceptable.
